name: Tharwa Valley Forge
specialty: Hand-forged knives and cutlery; largest knifemaking/bladesmithing school of its type, teaching forging, Damascus, Japanese and folding knives
note: Their own team page (Tharwa ACT) lists founder Karim Haddad and 12+ bladesmith/cutler instructors.

name: Alistair Phillips Folders
specialty: Custom folding knives (framelock/flipper), hand and CNC; collaborated with Spyderco on the Kapara
note: His own site confirms Canberra-based folding-knife specialist; AKG full member and VP, teaches folder courses at Tharwa Valley Forge.

name: Kev's Forge
specialty: Hand-forged chef and hunting/utility knives; award-winning bladesmith Kevin Slattery
note: Own site and Nordic Edge maker profile confirm South Canberra workshop with forge-to-finish knifemaking classes.

name: Tansu Knives
specialty: High-end Japanese-style kitchen knives (gyuto, wa-handle, san-mai) by Mert Tansu, ex-chef
note: Directory and Newcastle Herald/Nordic Edge profiles confirm Hunter Valley NSW maker; also teaches Japanese kitchen-knife workshops.

name: Maher Knives
specialty: Aesthetic custom knives made by hand by Craig Maher; internationally collected
note: Listed in Aussie knifemakers directory (Hunter Valley) and Newcastle Herald as Branxton bladesmith; AKG full member #124. Own domain no longer resolves.

name: Oatley Knives
specialty: Hand-forged Japanese-style chef knives (gyuto, sujihiki, honesuki, nakiri) with custom sayas by James Oatley
note: Directory lists Oatley Knives in Sydney; Eatingtools profile confirms St Leonards, Sydney bladesmith.

name: Brook Turner Blades
specialty: Custom gyuto/chef knives with clean finishes; one of Australia's most respected makers
note: Directory and The Blade Runner profile place him on Sydney's Northern Beaches; part-time bladesmith taking custom orders.

name: DP Custom Knives
specialty: High-performance handmade chef knives by Dmitriy Popov; one-man workshop
note: Own about page and Eatingtools profile confirm NSW maker who moved to the Central Coast; Knife Art Association member.

name: Keith Fludder Knives
specialty: ABS Journeyman Smith; custom chef, pocket, survival and tactical knives
note: Directory and his own site confirm Tahmoor NSW; teaches knifemaking in Sydney and co-founded the Knife Art Association/Sydney Knife Show.

name: Sacred Blades
specialty: Bushcraft, outdoor and kitchen knives; ex-chef bladesmith Cameron Elder
note: Own site confirms Sydney NSW bladesmith Cameron Elder with a shop page.

name: KP Custom Knives
specialty: Handmade kitchen knives for chefs, restaurants and home cooks
note: Own site confirms Sydney-based handmade kitchen-knife maker with shop and custom-knife form.

name: Lucid Knives
specialty: Hand-made custom kitchen knives forged in the Hunter Valley
note: Own site and search results confirm custom kitchen knives forged in Hunter Valley/Newcastle NSW.

name: Iron Gate Knives
specialty: Handmade bushcraft, saltwater-fishing and kitchen knives with antler/abalone/hardwood handles
note: Own site confirms a four-maker coastal workshop in Anna Bay NSW with weekly online drops and custom orders.

name: Ironlord Forge
specialty: Hand-forged knives, axes and ironwork by Wayne Saunders; full-time blacksmith/knifemaker since 2003
note: Own site confirms Wauchope (Mid North Coast) NSW forge; AKG full member offering knifemaking/Damascus courses.

name: Adamas Forge
specialty: Handcrafted forged kitchen and utility knives by Joel Davey; stocked by Hunter Valley restaurants
note: Own about page and Newcastle Herald confirm Bennetts Green, Lake Macquarie NSW; recently accepted into the AKG and runs knifemaking workshops.

name: Bat Country Forge
specialty: Handmade bushcraft, kitchen (San Mai chef) knives and traditional Scottish blades
note: Own site confirms Newcastle-area forge (began in Raymond Terrace NSW) with knife/blacksmith classes and an online store.

name: Mother Mountain Forge
specialty: Hand-forged Damascus knives for hunters and cooks, plus decorative blacksmithing (Iain & Sarah Hamilton)
note: Own site and Narooma News confirm a far-south-coast NSW forge at Dignams Creek running by-appointment knifemaking courses.

name: Jurkijevic Bladesmiths
specialty: Hand-forged hunting, chef's and ceremonial knives and custom tools; heirloom quality (Karl & Simeon Jurkijevic)
note: Own site confirms hand-forged custom knives and weekend knifemaking classes with Karl; Australian bladesmiths.

name: Nordic Edge
specialty: Forge-your-own chef-knife workshops (max 4 students) plus blacksmithing/spoon-carving classes and supplies (Bjorn Jacobsen)
note: Own site and Manly Observer confirm Brookvale, Sydney workshop founded by bladesmith/instructor Bjorn Jacobsen.

name: Eveleigh Works
specialty: Blacksmithing, knifemaking and toolmaking classes in a heritage locomotive workshop
note: Own site confirms Eveleigh, Sydney NSW; runs beginner and advanced knife-making courses taught by blacksmiths/knifemakers.

name: David Graham Simply Forged
specialty: Forged custom ironwork and knives
note: Listed as a NSW maker (Forged Custom Ironworks, simplyforged.com.au) in the Artisan Supplies teachers/makers directory.

name: 13 Knives
specialty: Hand-forged custom kitchen knives, homewares and champagne sabers (Bud Heyser)
note: Own site: Bud Heyser hand-forges custom knives in Collingwood, Melbourne, with a commission page and online shop.

name: Cut Throat Knives
specialty: Bespoke handmade kitchen/culinary knives (Indigo Roberts)
note: Own site: Melbourne bladesmith with a Coburg North store, 'Blade Lab' knife-making classes and custom/restaurant orders.

name: MD Knives
specialty: Handmade Western and Japanese-style chef/kitchen knives (Mathieu Dechamps)
note: Mathieu Dechamps hand-makes custom kitchen knives in his Dandenong Ranges workshop, per Boss Hunting profile and mdknives.com.au.

name: McIntyre Knives
specialty: Forged chef knives and cutting tools, own Damascus; ABS Master Smith Shawn McIntyre
note: Eatingtools profile: Master Smith forging world-class chef knives and Damascus at his one-man Melbourne shop.

name: Peter Del Raso
specialty: Custom hunters, fighters, utility and kitchen knives, collector pieces (stock removal)
note: AKG profile / bladeforums: award-winning Melbourne maker and long-time Guild Victorian state rep working from his Melbourne workshop.

name: DB Blades
specialty: Handmade outdoor and custom fixed-blade knives (Dom Binkert)
note: Own site: full-time Melbourne maker Dom Binkert making small-batch handmade knives; Facebook post confirms Melbourne base. AKG member #144.

name: Parker Knives
specialty: Handmade hunters, kitchen knives, Bowies, folders; forged incl. Damascus (Adam Parker)
note: Visit Ballarat feature: 100% handmade custom knives on the family farm near Ballarat; runs 2-day knife-making courses. AKG member #21.

name: Will Morrison Knives
specialty: Handmade custom knives
note: Own site plus AKG Victorian membership (#54) and Melbourne knife-show coverage; exact town not stated on pages seen.

name: Wilkur Forge
specialty: Knifemaking, toolmaking and blacksmithing training; custom work (Jim Deering)
note: Own site: Jim Deering runs a training forge near Leongatha teaching knife making, plus commissions.

name: Waterside Metal Art
specialty: Hand-forged knife-making courses; resident bladesmith custom knives
note: Own site: Melbourne inner-west artist-blacksmith studio; knifemaker/bladesmith teaches custom knife courses.

name: Metal Monkey Knives
specialty: Japanese-inspired kitchen/chef knives, carbon steel, timber handles (Tobias Bockholt)
note: Bellarine Times profile: award-winning maker Tobi Bockholt, now based in Franklin, Southern Tasmania; own site offers courses and custom requests.

name: Forge+
specialty: Handmade custom knives; hands-on knifemaking courses
note: Own site: dedicated knifemaking workshop in the Huon Valley offering custom knives and hands-on classes for all levels.

name: Ellard Blacksmithing
specialty: High-end forged culinary blades and Damascus; forging tools (Jason Ellard)
note: Own site: young Tasmanian bladesmith in Coningham forging Damascus culinary knives; online shop and gallery.

name: Newham Knives
specialty: Handcrafted kitchen (gyuto, petty) and outdoor knives (Will Newham)
note: Own site: self-taught maker Will Newham handcrafts high-performance knives in Howden, Tasmania; online shop.

name: Kirkman Knives
specialty: Handmade knives with sustainably sourced Tasmanian timbers (Myles Kirkman)
note: Referenced in Tasmanian maker coverage as a Tasmanian handmade knife business; own site did not resolve when checked, so website left null.

name: Winter Forged
specialty: Hand-forged knives, high-carbon steel and stabilised specialty timbers
note: Facebook/search evidence: hand-crafted knives forged in Wattle Hill, Tasmania; own site with shop confirmed.

name: Organic Blades
specialty: Hand-forged kitchen and outdoor knives, salvaged Tasmanian timber and Damascus (Tim Lowry)
note: Own site: Tim Lowry forges, shapes and finishes all knives in-house in Tasmania with salvaged Tasmanian timbers.

name: John Hounslow-Robinson
specialty: Damascus steel bespoke knives from salvaged materials
note: Brand Tasmania feature: renowned Damascus knife maker forging in Southern Tasmania's Huon Valley; sought out by chefs like Tetsuya Wakuda. No own website confirmed.

name: Tom Hounslow Knives
specialty: Hand-forged Damascus and stainless kitchen knives
note: 2nd-generation Tasmanian bladesmith (son of John Hounslow-Robinson) making forged Damascus kitchen knives; Big Cartel shop is his own site.

name: Bismarck Metalcraft
specialty: Handcrafted knives and swords, hunting knives, art/fantasy knives, restoration (Ken Bradford)
note: Rediscover Tasmania / gallery site: Ken Bradford handcrafts knives and swords in his Deloraine (northern Tasmania) workshop with a public gallery.

name: Dog Boy Knives
specialty: Hand-forged high-carbon chef, Damascus and Sanmai kitchen knives from recycled steel (Richard Cooper)
note: About page confirms Richard Cooper hand-forges knives in a Brompton SA workshop with an online shop and commissions.

name: Gardner Knives
specialty: Handmade kitchen and hunting knives (Barry Gardner, Barossa Valley)
note: Own site confirms family knifemaking business in the Barossa running workshops, commissions and The Tiny Knife Shop.

name: Riflebird Knives
specialty: Custom field and kitchen knives (hunting, fishing, utility, cooking) (Warrick Edmonds)
note: Own site confirms AKG member Warrick Edmonds makes custom knives in the Adelaide Hills and exhibits at the Adelaide Knife Show.

name: Astley Wright Knives
specialty: Hand-forged Damascus, high-carbon and stainless chef, kitchen and utility knives (Phil Astley)
note: Own site describes Phil Astley making knives at his Hill Street Shed in Adelaide and running knifemaking workshops.

name: Sam Heath Knives
specialty: Handcrafted chef's knives forged from scratch with Australian-sourced handles
note: Own site and Bowerbird market listing confirm ex-chef Sam Heath forges chef knives in Adelaide SA.

name: Ryan Simon Knives
specialty: Bowie, hunting, EDC/folding and chef knives incl. Damascus
note: Own About page confirms AKG full member Ryan Simon makes knives in Adelaide SA, takes commissions and teaches (Shingleback Knives).

name: Hahndorf Knife Shop
specialty: Fully hand-forged kitchen knives (Nathan Roennfeldt / Naroe Blades)
note: Shop site confirms AKG member Nathan (Naroe Blades) hand-forges kitchen knives at the family shop at 71 Main St, Hahndorf SA.

name: Zo Blades
specialty: Custom handmade kitchen, chef, hunting, pocket and Damascus/sanmai knives
note: Own site describes Zohar making custom handmade knives in Prospect, Adelaide SA, shipping Australia-wide.

name: Fallen Forge
specialty: Hand-crafted kitchen/chef, outdoor utility and Damascus knives, made to order (Rian Doudle)
note: Own site confirms Adelaide SA veteran-run forge making made-to-order knives and running knife-making workshops.

name: Ben Cutts Knife Works
specialty: Japanese-inspired handcrafted knives
note: Directory and Adelaide Knife Show listings confirm AKG probationary member Ben Cutts, an artisan cutler from the Riverland SA; no own website found.

name: GT Edgeworks
specialty: Custom culinary/chef knives (Josh Gaunt)
note: Nordic Edge Adelaide Knife Show coverage confirms Josh Gaunt of GT Edgeworks makes custom culinary knives in SA.

name: Celery Patch Knives
specialty: Custom handmade chef's knives in stainless, high-carbon and Damascus (Ash Edwards)
note: Adelaide Knife Show coverage names Ash Edwards of Celery Patch Knives as an SA maker; no standalone maker website confirmed.

name: Kieran Swift
specialty: Forged decorative Damascus custom knives
note: AKG committee page and Border Watch article confirm Adelaide-based full guild member Kieran Swift making decorative Damascus knives since 2018; no own website found.

name: Anthony Kittel Knives
specialty: Custom handmade knives
note: Downunder knifemakers directory lists Anthony Kittel as a South Australian custom knifemaker; no own website found.

name: DOCO Knives
specialty: Hand-forged Damascus, kitchen, hunting and camp knives with WA hardwood handles (Tony Docherty)
note: Own site confirms Tony 'Doco' Docherty hand-forges knives and runs knife-making workshops in the Fremantle/Perth WA area.

name: MB Bladesmith
specialty: Hand-forged high-carbon kitchen knives (Mark Barrett)
note: Own site confirms Mark Barrett hand-forges kitchen knives in Perth WA and takes custom orders.

name: David Brodziak Custom Knives
specialty: Fine custom knives incl. art/collaborative painted pieces and fishing knives
note: Own site and search confirm award-winning full-time maker David Brodziak has made knives in Albany WA since 1989.

name: Barnett Custom Knives
specialty: Pattern-welded/feather Damascus bowies, fighters, slipjoints, hunters and chef knives (Bruce Barnett)
note: Knife-show profile confirms AKG member Bruce Barnett works at Windy Hollow Forge, Bridgetown WA, running folder courses; old domain now redirects to spam so website is null.

name: Barking Gecko Forge
specialty: Hunter, kitchen and slipjoint folder knifemaking (stock-removal and forged)
note: Own classes page confirms a knifemaking forge/school in Nannup, southwest WA, running 1-3 day knifemaking courses.

name: BF Bladeworks
specialty: Hand-forged steak knives, kiridashi and blacksmith-made blades
note: Own site confirms a Perth WA blacksmith/knifemaker hand-forging knives with an Etsy shop and gallery.

name: Brook Knives
specialty: Handmade chef/kitchen knives marked 'Brook Australia' (Matt Brook)
note: Directory and blade-forum posts confirm AKG full member Matt Brook makes knives in Perth WA; sold via The Blade Runner, no confirmed own site.

name: Tufnell Custom Knives
specialty: Hunting, pocket, biltong, carving and steak knives, in-house heat-treated (Ron Tufnell)
note: Sporting Shooter reviews confirm AKG member Ron Tufnell handcrafts hunting knives in Broome WA; no own website confirmed.

name: Chris Roosendaal
specialty: Custom knives (hunting-oriented)
note: AKG member list and profile confirm full guild member Chris Roosendaal making knives in WA after training with Bruce Barnett; no own website found.

name: Sinclair Knives Australia
specialty: Custom handmade knives (Mark Sinclair)
note: AKG member list places full member Mark Sinclair (#70) in WA; a Sinclair Knives site exists but content could not be verified so website left null.

name: Thomas Gerner
specialty: Forged blades and barrel knives; Australia's first ABS Master Bladesmith
note: Adelaide Knife Show post and multiple sources confirm WA-based Thomas Gerner, Australia's first Master Bladesmith, producing forged knives.

name: Karim Haddad / Tharwa Valley Forge
specialty: Bladesmithing school and production forge; hand-forged knives, courses since 2003
note: Own site confirms Tharwa ACT address, courses, retail shop and production cutlery.

name: James Oatley / Oatley Knives
specialty: Japanese-style single-bevel chef knives (gyuto, sushi, honesuki)

specialty: High-end Japanese-style chef knives; also runs classes
